parameters.text:
=You must classify the following name and respond ONLY with valid JSON.

NAME: {{ $json.rawClientName }}

Is this a COMPANY name or a PERSON name?

Rules:
- If the name contains LLC, Inc, Ltd, Corp, GmbH, SAS, Agency, Studio, Group, Labs, Solutions, Technologies, Services, Consulting, Co., or any business suffix → it is a COMPANY
- If the name looks like a first name + last name (e.g. "John Smith", "Ahmed Ben Yahia") → it is a PERSON
- If ambiguous, lean toward COMPANY
- If rawClientName is null or empty → respond with type: "unknown"

Respond ONLY with this exact JSON, no markdown:
{"type": "company", "name": "{{ $json.rawClientName }}"}  
or  
{"type": "person", "name": "{{ $json.rawClientName }}"}

options.systemMessage: You are a name classifier. You respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation, no markdown, just the JSON object.
You are a business intelligence analyst. Given context about an Upwork client, you identify the company they represent. Respond ONLY with valid JSON.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"companyName": "Acme Corp", "confidence": "high", "reasoning": "Job descriptions mention Acme Corp branding"}

body.jobTitle: Full stack and Web app developer
body.description:
Full-Stack Web & Mobile Developer (Contract – 12 Weeks)

We are building a production-ready web application with a clear scope, defined requirements, and a fixed 12-week delivery timeline.
